title: BenchmarkRunStarted payload
description: Emitted at the start of a benchmark run. The provenance bundle is fully populated at this point — every component needed to replay the run identically (T6's first-class replay operation) is captured here, not deferred to BenchmarkRunCompleted. A run that starts but never emits Completed/Failed (e.g. process killed mid-run) is detectable by absence; provenance is preserved for the partial run regardless.

scenario_id.description: Which scenario / rubric is being run. Matches the existing benchmark_results.scenario_id / task_id concept.

provenance.description: Fixed-shape provenance bundle. Every field required — a benchmark run that can't pin one of these is not replayable and shouldn't be recorded as a benchmark (it should be a different event type, or upstream code should fix the gap). T6 detail-filled the T1 draft: added retriever_config_hash (distinct from retriever_version — version pins which retriever, config_hash pins its runtime knobs). The envelope's event_time captures wall-clock-start; BenchmarkRunCompleted.wall_clock_ms captures wall-clock-end, so neither lives here as a payload field.

model_id.description: Model identifier — e.g. 'claude-opus-4-7', 'qwen2.5-32b'. The user-facing model name.

model_version.description: Specific model version / weights identifier. For Anthropic models: the full versioned ID (e.g. 'claude-opus-4-7-20260301'). For local Qwen: the GGUF file hash or Ollama digest.

prompt_template_hash.description: Hex SHA-256 of the prompt template used for this scenario. Pinning prompt content; a template edit produces a different hash and thus a different replay.

corpus_hash.description: Hex SHA-256 of the corpus content used (vault snapshot, kiwix ZIM version, scenario fixture set). Pins the retrieval-time data.

retriever_version.description: Version identifier for the retriever (e.g. 'qwen-rerank-2.5-32b-v3', 'fts5-default'). A retriever change shifts what gets returned for a query.

retriever_config_hash.description: Hex SHA-256 over the retriever's runtime configuration knobs (top-k, similarity threshold, reranker temperature, score-fusion weights). Distinct from retriever_version: same version with different config produces different rankings, so config must be pinned for replay determinism. For retrievers with no tunable knobs, use the SHA-256 of an empty JSON object '{}' as a canonical sentinel.

seed.description: RNG seed. Required for replay determinism. For models that don't accept a seed (Anthropic API), record 0 and document the non-determinism in the model_id metadata.

env_hash.description: Hex SHA-256 over the runtime environment fingerprint (binary version, library versions, platform). Detects 'replayed on a different machine and got a different answer' drift.
